10.reason: [Missing address-of operator (&) before 'a'. Should be scanf("%d", &a); This causes undefined behavior and likely a runtime error.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing address-of operator (&) before 'a'. Should be scanf("%d", &a); This causes undefined behavior and likely a runtime error.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing & before a, should be scanf("%d", &a);
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing ampersand: should be scanf("%d", &a);
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing address-of operator: should be &a, causing undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The scanf function requires the address of the variable 'a' to store the input. It should be '&a' instead of 'a'. Passing 'a' (which is uninitialized or holds garbage value) as an address leads to undefined behavior and likely a segmentation fault (Runtime Error).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: scanf requires the address of the variable 'a' to store the input. It should be '&a'. Passing 'a' (which is uninitialized or garbage value) as an address causes a segmentation fault/runtime error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The variable 'a' is passed to scanf without the address-of operator (&), causing undefined behavior. It should be '&a'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing '&' before variable 'a'; should be '&a' to pass address to scanf]
